items.question: How many halves make up a standard soccer match?
items.explanation: A standard match is split into two halves.

items.question: How many players from one team are usually on the field in soccer, including the goalkeeper?
items.explanation: A standard side fields 11 players including the goalkeeper.

items.question: How many points is a goal worth in soccer?
items.explanation: Each goal counts as one score in soccer.

items.question: What color card sends a player off?
items.explanation: A red card means the player is sent off.

items.question: What happens when a player is offside at the moment a teammate plays the ball and then becomes involved?
items.correctAnswer: Play is stopped for an offside offense
incorrectAnswers: A penalty kick is awarded automatically
The player is cautioned every time
items.explanation: Offside requires involvement in active play before it is penalized.

items.question: What is the standard length of a regulation soccer match before extra time?
items.explanation: A standard match has two 45-minute halves.

items.question: What restart follows when the ball fully crosses the touchline after last touching the defending team?
items.correctAnswer: A throw-in for the attacking team
items.explanation: Crossing the touchline leads to a throw-in for the team that did not touch it last.

items.question: What restart is awarded when the defending team last touches the ball before it crosses its own goal line without a goal?
items.explanation: An attacking team gets a corner when defenders touch it last over their own goal line.

items.question: Where is a penalty kick taken from?
incorrectAnswers: The edge of the center line
items.explanation: Penalty kicks are taken from the penalty mark inside the penalty area.

items.question: Which player is allowed to handle the ball inside that player's own penalty area?
items.explanation: Only the goalkeeper may use hands within the team's own penalty area under the laws.
